name: Georgia SNAP (Food Stamps)
description: Federally-funded program providing monthly benefits to low-income households to help pay for the cost of food. Apply online through Georgia Gateway.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% of federal poverty level; net income at or below 100% FPL
hours: Georgia Gateway available Mon-Fri 5am-midnight

name: Georgia Gateway - Online Benefits Portal
description: Online portal for applying to and managing SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, CAPS childcare, and other public assistance programs in Georgia.
eligibilitySummary: Georgia residents applying for public assistance programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Mon-Fri 5:00 AM - midnight

name: Georgia Medicaid
description: State health coverage program for low-income individuals, families, pregnant women, elderly, and people with disabilities. Apply through Georgia Gateway.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, parents/caretakers, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by category; children up to 247% FPL via PeachCare
hours: Apply online 24/7 via Georgia Gateway; DFCS offices Mon-Fri

name: PeachCare for Kids
description: Low-cost health coverage for uninsured children in Georgia who do not qualify for Medicaid, providing medical, dental, and vision coverage.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 247% FPL (up to $81,510/year for a family of 4 in 2026); premiums $0-$35/month
hours: Apply online 24/7 via Georgia Gateway

name: Georgia Access - Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Georgia's state-based health insurance marketplace where residents can compare and enroll in affordable health insurance plans with potential subsidies.
eligibilitySummary: Georgia residents seeking individual health insurance coverage
incomeLimitNotes: Subsidies available based on income; varies by household size
hours: Open enrollment annually Nov-Jan; special enrollment year-round for qualifying events

name: Georgia Department of Labor - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost jobs through no fault of their own, earned sufficient wages, and are actively seeking work
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned at least $1,890 in highest quarter of base period; weekly benefit up to $365
hours: Phone: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM ET; Online claims 24/7

name: MyUI Claimant Portal - Georgia Unemployment
description: Online portal for filing unemployment insurance claims, requesting weekly benefit payments, viewing determinations, and managing your unemployment case.
eligibilitySummary: Georgia workers filing for or receiving unemployment benefits
hours: Available 24/7 online

name: Georgia Department of Community Affairs (DCA) - Housing
description: State housing finance agency administering affordable housing programs including Housing Choice Vouchers, rental assistance, and homeownership programs.
address: 60 Executive Park South NE, Atlanta, GA 30329
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families seeking affordable housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Housing Choice Voucher: income at or below 50% area median income; preference for extremely low income (30% AMI)
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Georgia Housing Choice Voucher Program (Section 8)
description: Tenant-based rental assistance helping very low-income families afford safe, decent housing in the private rental market.
address: 60 Executive Park South NE, Atlanta, GA 30329
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income individuals and families; 75% of vouchers reserved for extremely low-income applicants
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 50% of area median income
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Georgia Rental Assistance Program
description: State program providing emergency rental assistance to individuals and families facing housing instability.
eligibilitySummary: Georgia residents experiencing financial hardship affecting ability to pay rent
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility varies; generally at or below 80% area median income
hours: Call or text 833-827-RENT

name: Georgia Legal Services Program (GLSP)
description: Nonprofit providing free civil legal services to low-income individuals in 154 Georgia counties outside metro Atlanta, covering housing, family law, consumer, and public benefits issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in Georgia's 154 non-metro-Atlanta counties with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Atlanta Legal Aid Society
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income residents in Clayton, Cobb, DeKalb, Fulton, and Gwinnett Counties covering housing, family, public benefits, and consumer law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Clayton, Cobb, DeKalb, Fulton, and Gwinnett Counties with civil legal problems
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level
hours: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: GeorgiaLegalAid.org
description: Joint project of Atlanta Legal Aid and Georgia Legal Services providing free online legal information, self-help tools, and referrals to legal services statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Any Georgia resident seeking legal information or referrals
hours: Website available 24/7

name: Georgia Senior Legal Aid
description: Free legal services for Georgia residents aged 60 and older, covering issues such as elder abuse, housing, healthcare, and consumer protection.
eligibilitySummary: Georgia residents aged 60 and older
hours: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: WorkSource Georgia - Workforce Development
description: Network of American Job Centers providing free job search assistance, career counseling, training programs, resume services, and employment support across Georgia.
eligibilitySummary: All Georgia job seekers and workers; priority for veterans, low-income individuals, and dislocated workers
incomeLimitNotes: Free services for all; WIOA training funds have income eligibility requirements
hours: Varies by location; typically Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: WorkSource Metro Atlanta
description: Hub of free employment resources for job seekers in the 10-county metro Atlanta area, offering job search tools, career coaching, training, and placement services.
eligibilitySummary: Job seekers in the 10-county metro Atlanta region
hours: Available 24/7 online; career center hours vary

name: Georgia LIHEAP - Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program
description: Federally-funded program providing financial assistance to low-income households for home heating and cooling energy costs, crisis assistance, and weatherization.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households responsible for paying home energy bills; priority for elderly (65+) and medically homebound
incomeLimitNotes: Household gross income at or below 60% of State Median Income
hours: Heating: opens December; Cooling: opens April for seniors/disabled, May for all others

name: Georgia Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to homes of low-income residents to reduce energy costs and improve comfort and safety.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters; priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income cannot exceed 200% of federal poverty level
hours: Contact local Community Action Agency for hours

name: Georgia 211 - United Way
description: Free, confidential helpline connecting Georgians to essential community resources including food, housing, healthcare, employment, and crisis services.
eligibilitySummary: All Georgia residents seeking information about community services and resources
hours: 24/7; text ZIP code and need to 898211

name: United Way of Greater Atlanta 211
description: Searchable online database and helpline connecting metro Atlanta residents to local social services including food, shelter, healthcare, and financial assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Metro Atlanta residents seeking community resources
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM for phone; database available 24/7

name: Atlanta Community Food Bank
description: Fourth-largest food bank in the U.S. distributing over 112 million meals annually through 700+ partner agencies across 29 counties in metro Atlanta and north Georgia.
eligibilitySummary: Food-insecure individuals and families in 29 counties across metro Atlanta and north Georgia
hours: Community Food Centers require appointments; call 478-394-4547 to schedule

name: Second Harvest of South Georgia
description: Leading hunger-relief organization and largest rural food bank in Georgia, serving 26 counties in south Georgia through food distribution and nutrition programs.
address: Valdosta, GA (headquarters with branches in Thomasville and Tifton)
eligibilitySummary: Food-insecure individuals and families in 26 counties across south Georgia
hours: Contact for distribution schedules

name: Middle Georgia Community Food Bank
description: Regional food bank serving 24 counties in middle Georgia through over 140 partner food pantries, providing equitable access to nutritious food.
eligibilitySummary: Food-insecure individuals and families in 24 counties across middle Georgia
hours: Contact for distribution schedules

name: Food Bank of Northeast Georgia
description: Regional food bank serving 15 counties in northeast Georgia through partnerships with over 230 member nonprofits, distributing 15.5 million pounds of food annually.
eligibilitySummary: Food-insecure individuals and families in 15 counties across northeast Georgia
hours: Contact for distribution schedules

name: Second Harvest of Coastal Georgia
description: Regional food bank serving coastal Georgia communities with food distribution and hunger relief programs as part of the Feeding America network.
eligibilitySummary: Food-insecure individuals and families in coastal Georgia counties
hours: Contact for distribution schedules

name: Feeding Georgia
description: Statewide association of seven regional food banks fighting food insecurity across Georgia, distributing over 180 million pounds of food through 2,000+ partner agencies annually.
eligibilitySummary: All Georgians experiencing food insecurity; use website to find nearest food bank
hours: Use website to locate nearest food bank and distribution site

name: Georgia TANF - Temporary Assistance for Needy Families
description: Monthly cash assistance program with employment services for low-income families with children under 18. Includes work requirements and job training.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 18 (or 19 if full-time student) and pregnant women
incomeLimitNotes: Family of 3: gross income below $784/month; countable assets under $1,000
hours: Apply online via Georgia Gateway or at local DFCS office Mon-Fri

name: Georgia CAPS - Childcare and Parent Services
description: Subsidized childcare program helping eligible parents access affordable childcare so they can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Parents who are working, in school, or in training and need childcare assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility based on family size and federal poverty guidelines
hours: Apply online via Georgia Gateway; helpline Mon-Fri

name: Georgia WIC - Women, Infants, and Children
description: Nutrition program providing nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant/postpartum women, infants, and children under 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level
hours: Services at local health departments; hours vary by location

name: Georgia Head Start
description: Free early childhood education program for eligible children from birth to age 5, providing comprehensive education, health, nutrition, and family support services.
eligibilitySummary: Children birth to age 5 from low-income families; children in foster care, homeless families, and families receiving TANF
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 100% of federal poverty level
hours: Program hours vary by location; typically follows school year calendar

name: MARTA Reduced Fare Program
description: Discounted transit fares on all MARTA buses and trains for seniors aged 65+, people with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders in metro Atlanta.
address: Five Points Rail Station, 30 Alabama Street SW, Atlanta, GA 30303
eligibilitySummary: Seniors aged 65+, persons with documented physical or mental disability, or Medicare cardholders
hours: Reduced fare Breeze Cards available at Five Points Station and MARTA HQ during business hours

name: MARTA Mobility (Paratransit)
description: Door-to-door shared-ride transit service for individuals with disabilities who are unable to use fixed-route MARTA bus and rail service.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with disabilities who cannot use regular MARTA bus and rail service; must apply and be certified
hours: Service available during MARTA operating hours

name: Georgia Department of Veterans Service
description: State agency providing benefits counseling, claims assistance, education and training support, and referrals for Georgia veterans and their families.
address: Floyd Veterans Memorial Building, Suite E-970, Atlanta, GA 30334
eligibilitySummary: Georgia veterans, their dependents, and survivors
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Veterans Crisis Line: dial 988, press 1 (24/7)

name: Georgia Vocational Rehabilitation Agency (GVRA)
description: State agency helping people with disabilities find employment through vocational counseling, job training, assistive technology, and placement services.
address: 200 Piedmont Ave SE, Suite 1410, Atlanta, GA 30334
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with permanent physical, mental, or emotional disabilities that create barriers to employment
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Georgia Crisis and Access Line (GCAL)
description: 24/7 crisis hotline for Georgians experiencing mental health, substance use, or developmental disability crises. Also answers 988 calls in Georgia.
eligibilitySummary: Any Georgian experiencing a mental health, substance use, or developmental disability crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Georgia
description: National crisis line with Georgia-specific response through the Georgia Crisis and Access Line (GCAL), providing immediate support for suicidal thoughts and emotional distress.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Georgia experiencing suicidal thoughts, emotional distress, or mental health crisis
hours: 24/7 by phone, text, or chat

name: ATL311 - City of Atlanta Services
description: Atlanta's primary phone number and website for accessing city government services, reporting issues, and connecting to social services and community resources.
eligibilitySummary: City of Atlanta residents and visitors needing government services or information
hours: 24/7 for online; phone hours vary

name: Fulton County Human Services
description: Fulton County programs connecting residents to health, housing, employment, and social services through the Fulton Cares app and community service network.
eligibilitySummary: Fulton County residents seeking health, housing, employment, or social services
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Fulton County DFCS
description: Fulton County office of the Division of Family and Children Services, providing SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, childcare assistance, and protective services.
address: 1249 Donald Lee Hollowell Pkwy NW, Atlanta, GA 30318
eligibilitySummary: Fulton County residents applying for public assistance programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Georgia Community Action Association
description: Statewide network of 20 Community Action Agencies providing comprehensive services including energy assistance, housing, employment, and emergency aid across all 159 Georgia counties.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families; use website to find local Community Action Agency
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125-200% of federal poverty level depending on program
hours: Varies by local agency

name: Goodwill of North Georgia - Career Services
description: Free career centers across north Georgia providing job search assistance, resume help, skills training, career coaching, and employment placement services.
eligibilitySummary: All job seekers in north Georgia; no income requirements for career center services
hours: 14 career centers; hours vary by location

name: Georgia Division of Aging Services
description: State agency coordinating services for older Georgians including meals, transportation, caregiver support, case management, and connections to Area Agencies on Aging.
eligibilitySummary: Georgia residents aged 60 and older; caregivers of older adults
incomeLimitNotes: Many services available regardless of income; some programs have income criteria
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; contact local Area Agency on Aging

name: Grady Health System
description: Atlanta's safety-net hospital providing healthcare to uninsured and underinsured residents of Fulton and DeKalb counties, including emergency, primary, and specialty care.
address: 80 Jesse Hill Jr Dr SE, Atlanta, GA 30303
eligibilitySummary: All patients; financial assistance available for uninsured/underinsured Fulton and DeKalb county residents
incomeLimitNotes: Financial assistance based on income and residency in Fulton or DeKalb County
hours: Emergency department open 24/7; clinic hours vary

name: Georgia Department of Human Services - DFCS
description: State agency administering SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, childcare assistance, child protective services, and other social services programs through county offices statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Georgia residents in need of public assistance, child welfare, or adult protective services
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program
hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Georgia Relay for hearing impaired: 800-255-0135
